title: fix: sync renamed GitHub usernames
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Sync GitHub usernames twice daily in 100-user GraphQL batches.
- Update only changed D1 usernames in write batches.
- Fixes #12456.

gist.headline: Names that keep blooming
gist.blurb: GitHub name changes now find their way back to the hive twice a day. Less stale identity pollen, more correctly labeled contributors.
gist.summary: Added a twice-daily GitHub Actions sync that fetches GitHub usernames in 100-user GraphQL batches and updates only changed usernames in D1 write batches.
gist.impact: Contributors who rename their GitHub accounts will see their updated username reflected across Pollinations data without manual cleanup.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy contributor garden where name tags on blooming code-plants are gently refreshed: Polly, wearing a tiny gardener hat, compares a GitHub-style profile card against a neat D1 seed ledger while a CRT monitor robot helps stamp updated labels. The repeated batch process is represented by orderly trays of 100 tiny seed packets, with only the changed labels being replanted.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener hat, carefully replacing a changed username label on a blooming code-plant while a chunky CRT monitor robot holds a GitHub-style profile card beside a small seed ledger; neat trays of tiny labeled seeds suggest batched syncing. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, subtle pixel-font text: 'SYNCED'.

Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, subtle pixel-font text: 'SYNCED'.
